outcomes.description: Browse, create, and update items in the system, and monitor the audit log for all recorded actions.

scenarios.scenario: View the item list and gateway status
scenarios.description: User lands on the Item Management page and reviews all items stored in the database, with each item showing its name, description, and completion state. The gateway connection status is displayed at the top. Data loads automatically on page open and reloads whenever the active persona changes.

actions.action: Select the active persona for all requests
actions.description: label: Persona; type: enum; options: Admin, User, Viewer; default: Admin; changing the selection updates the X-Persona header sent with all subsequent API calls and triggers an automatic reload of items, audit log, and gateway status; visibleTo: all personas

actions.action: Request the item list from the server
actions.description: Triggered automatically on page open and on persona change; sends X-Persona: {persona} header; also refreshed after item creation and status updates

apis.response: array of item objects (id, name, description, completed)

actions.description: Triggered automatically on page open and on persona change; sends X-Persona: {persona} header; on fetch failure sets status to 'Error connecting to gateway'

steps.step: Review the gateway status and item list

actions.description: label: Gateway Status; source: GET / response.message field; formatting: displayed as plain text in the status card header; emptyState: 'Error connecting to gateway' shown on fetch failure
label: Name; source: item.name; formatting: displayed as bold text within each list row; emptyState: 'No items found.' shown when the items array is empty
label: Description; source: item.description; formatting: displayed as plain text following a colon after the item name; emptyState: 'No items found.' when the items array is empty

label: Completion status; source: item.completed (boolean); formatting: CSS class 'completed' added to the list item element when true; also determines the control label shown in the row ('Complete' when false, 'Undo' when true)

scenarios.description: User fills in the item name and description and submits the form to create a new item. Both fields are required; blank values are rejected client-side before the request is sent. The form is visible to non-Viewer personas; User has access.

actions.description: label: Name; type: text; required: true; validation: value must not be blank after whitespace trimming (client-side guard before POST); placeholder: 'Name'; visibleTo: non-Viewer personas (User is included)

label: Description; type: text; required: true; validation: value must not be blank after whitespace trimming (client-side guard before POST); placeholder: 'Description'; visibleTo: non-Viewer personas (User is included)

actions.action: Submit the new item to the server
actions.description: Sends name and description as URL-encoded query parameters via POST; on HTTP error (non-2xx response) shows alert 'Error creating item: Unauthorized'; on success clears both fields and triggers a reload of the item list and audit log

apis.request: header: X-Persona: {persona}; query params: name (URL-encoded string), description (URL-encoded string)
apis.response: created item object; HTTP non-OK response triggers alert 'Error creating item: Unauthorized'

scenarios.scenario: Mark an item as complete or incomplete
scenarios.description: User selects the Complete or Undo control on an item row to toggle its completion state. The control label reflects the item's current state ('Complete' when incomplete, 'Undo' when complete). Available to non-Viewer personas; User has access.

steps.step: Choose the completion state to apply

actions.action: Indicate whether to complete or undo the item
actions.description: Control label shows 'Complete' when item.completed is false and 'Undo' when item.completed is true; selecting it inverts the current completion state and triggers the status update request; visibleTo: non-Viewer personas (User is included)

actions.action: Submit the updated completion status to the server
actions.description: Sends the toggled boolean value (opposite of current item.completed) as a query param via PUT; on HTTP error shows alert 'Error updating item: Unauthorized'; on success triggers a reload of the item list and audit log

apis.request: header: X-Persona: {persona}; path param: id (item integer ID); query param: completed (boolean, toggled from current item.completed value)
apis.response: HTTP 2xx on success; non-OK response triggers alert 'Error updating item: Unauthorized'

scenarios.description: User views the audit log section displaying all recorded system actions with their timestamp, action type, affected table name, and affected record ID. The log is loaded automatically on page open and refreshed after each item mutation.

steps.step: Load the audit log from the server

actions.action: Request the audit log entries from the server
actions.description: Triggered automatically on page open, on persona change, and after each item creation or status update; sends X-Persona: {persona} header

apis.response: array of audit log objects (id, created_at, action, table_name, record_id)

actions.description: label: Timestamp; source: log.created_at; formatting: converted via new Date(log.created_at).toLocaleString() to a localized date-time string; displayed as the first item in each log row; emptyState: 'No logs found.' when the audit log array is empty

actions.action: Observe each log entry's action type
actions.description: label: Action; source: log.action; formatting: displayed as plain text in the format '[{timestamp}] {action} on table {table_name} (Record ID: {record_id})' within each log row

actions.action: Observe each log entry's affected table
actions.description: label: Table; source: log.table_name; formatting: displayed as plain text in 'on table {log.table_name}' format within each log row

actions.action: Observe each log entry's affected record ID
actions.description: label: Record ID; source: log.record_id; formatting: displayed as plain text in '(Record ID: {log.record_id})' format at the end of each log row

skippedComponents.reason: React entry-point only — mounts App with StrictMode, no state or business logic
Pure stylesheet, no state or interactive logic
Pure global stylesheet, no state or interactive logic

skippedForVisibility.what: Delete item action (handleDelete — DELETE /api/items/{id}, rendered as Delete control in item row)
